name: Development with QA Gate
description: Standard workflow: Manager assigns task, Developer implements on feature branch, QA validates and merges to main.

ASSIGN.name: Task Assignment
ASSIGN.description: Manager routes the task specification to the developer without modification. The workflow engine handles routing mechanically. No decomposition or analysis—the developer receives the full specification and performs all analysis and decomposition.

IMPLEMENTING.name: Implementation
IMPLEMENTING.description: Developer analyzes the task specification, decomposes it into work items, and implements on a feature branch from latest main.
tasks: unit tests including round-trip (serialize/parse) tests for every public struct and enum, with 90%+ line coverage, covering every public API and error path
cargo tarpaulin coverage verification (90%+ line coverage)
validation that all unit tests pass
IMPLEMENTING.decompositionPrompt: Place test-writing work items AFTER implementation items but BEFORE any final verification step. Each test work item should reference the specific module and public items to test.

onEntryCommands.command: rm -f .git/index.lock .git/HEAD.lock 2>/dev/null; git merge --abort 2>/dev/null; git rebase --abort 2>/dev/null; git cherry-pick --abort 2>/dev/null; true
onEntryCommands.reason: Remove stale lock files and abort any stuck merge/rebase/cherry-pick from a prior LLM session.
Fetch latest remote tracking references.
Discard any uncommitted changes left by a prior LLM session.
Remove untracked files left by prior LLM session (reports, artifacts, etc.).
Switch to main branch.
Sync local main to latest origin/main before branching.

onEntryCommands.command: git checkout -b dev/{{taskId}} || git checkout dev/{{taskId}}
onEntryCommands.reason: Create the feature branch from main. If it already exists, switch to it.

onExitCommands.command: rm -f .git/index.lock .git/HEAD.lock 2>/dev/null; git merge --abort 2>/dev/null; git rebase --abort 2>/dev/null; git cherry-pick --abort 2>/dev/null; true
onExitCommands.reason: Remove stale lock files and abort any stuck merge/rebase/cherry-pick the LLM may have triggered.
Recover any changes the LLM may have stashed.
Auto-format code before commit. Failure must not block push.
Stage all new and modified files.
Commit staged changes if any exist.
Push current HEAD to origin dev/{{taskId}}. This MUST run so QA can see the code.
Capture the commit SHA for downstream traceability.

VALIDATING.name: QA Validation
VALIDATING.description: QA checks out the feature branch and runs build, test, and lint. Read-only validation only -- QA must not modify any source files.

onEntryCommands.command: rm -f .git/index.lock .git/HEAD.lock 2>/dev/null; git merge --abort 2>/dev/null; git rebase --abort 2>/dev/null; git cherry-pick --abort 2>/dev/null; true
onEntryCommands.reason: Remove stale lock files and abort any stuck merge/rebase/cherry-pick from a prior LLM session.
Fetch latest remote tracking references to see the developer's pushed branch.
Discard any uncommitted changes from a prior LLM session.
Remove untracked files (reports, artifacts) left by prior LLM session.

onEntryCommands.command: git checkout dev/{{taskId}} || git checkout -b dev/{{taskId}} origin/dev/{{taskId}}
onEntryCommands.reason: Switch to the feature branch. Create from origin if it does not exist locally.
Force-sync to origin/dev/{{taskId}} so you are validating exactly what the developer pushed.
Record the exact commit SHA being validated.
Mechanical fast-fail: reject immediately if pushed code is unformatted. Saves LLM invocation.
Mechanical fast-fail: reject immediately if pushed code does not build.
Mechanical fast-fail: reject immediately if pushed code fails tests.
Mechanical fast-fail: reject immediately if pushed code has lint warnings.

onExitCommands.reason: Capture the validated commit SHA for downstream traceability.

REWORK.name: Rework
REWORK.description: Developer fixes QA-reported issues on the same feature branch.
REWORK.prompt:
Fix the issues found by QA. You are ALREADY on branch dev/{{taskId}} -- the entry commands checked it out. Do NOT create branches, switch branches, or run git checkout.

**QA Rejection Reason:**
{{rejectionReason}}

The 'Agent notes' section in the prior workflow history above contains QA's detailed findings.

**Evaluate the findings first.** If you believe QA's rejection is incorrect or based on a misunderstanding:
- Run the quality gates yourself to confirm the code is correct
- Do NOT make unnecessary changes -- your summary note (captured automatically when this phase ends) will explain your reasoning to QA
- QA will see your note in the next validation cycle

If the findings are valid, address EACH specific issue.

**Procedure:**
1. cd {{projectPath}}
2. Address each valid issue from the QA findings (or confirm no changes needed)
3. Run: cargo fmt
4. Run: {{buildCommand}}
5. Run: {{testCommand}}
6. Run: {{lintCommand}}
7. Fix ALL errors -- do not proceed with failures

Do NOT call send_completion_report() or send_message() -- the workflow engine handles state transitions and routing automatically.

IMPORTANT: Do NOT create a new branch. Do NOT run git checkout. The exit commands will commit and push dev/{{taskId}} automatically.

onEntryCommands.command: rm -f .git/index.lock .git/HEAD.lock 2>/dev/null; git merge --abort 2>/dev/null; git rebase --abort 2>/dev/null; git cherry-pick --abort 2>/dev/null; true
onEntryCommands.reason: Remove stale lock files and abort any stuck merge/rebase/cherry-pick from a prior LLM session.
Fetch latest remote tracking references to see QA's validation evidence.
Discard any uncommitted changes from a prior LLM session.
Remove untracked files left by prior LLM session.
Switch to the feature branch.
Force-sync to origin/dev/{{taskId}} so you see the current code state.

onExitCommands.command: rm -f .git/index.lock .git/HEAD.lock 2>/dev/null; git merge --abort 2>/dev/null; git rebase --abort 2>/dev/null; git cherry-pick --abort 2>/dev/null; true
onExitCommands.reason: Remove stale lock files and abort any stuck merge/rebase/cherry-pick the LLM may have triggered.
Recover any changes the LLM may have stashed.
Auto-format rework code before commit. Failure must not block push.

onExitCommands.command: git diff --cached --quiet || git commit -m '{{role}}: {{state}} fix for {{taskId}}'
onExitCommands.reason: Commit rework fixes if any staged changes exist.
Push rework fixes to origin dev/{{taskId}}. This MUST run so QA can see the fixes.
Capture the commit SHA for downstream traceability.

MERGING.name: Merge to Main
MERGING.description: Fully mechanical: entry commands merge and push, exit commands capture SHA and delete branch. No LLM work needed.

onEntryCommands.command: rm -f .git/index.lock .git/HEAD.lock 2>/dev/null; git merge --abort 2>/dev/null; git rebase --abort 2>/dev/null; git cherry-pick --abort 2>/dev/null; true
onEntryCommands.reason: Remove stale lock files and abort any stuck merge/rebase/cherry-pick from a prior LLM session.
Fetch latest remote tracking references.
Discard any uncommitted changes from a prior LLM session.
Remove untracked files left by prior LLM session.

onEntryCommands.command: git checkout dev/{{taskId}} || git checkout -b dev/{{taskId}} origin/dev/{{taskId}}
onEntryCommands.reason: Checkout the feature branch locally. Create from origin if it does not exist locally (e.g., QA agent separate work dir).
Force-sync local feature branch to match origin exactly before rebasing.
Rebase the feature branch onto latest main with auto-resolution of safe additive conflicts (pub mod/use in lib.rs, deps in Cargo.toml). Falls back to abort and fail for non-trivial conflicts.
Switch to main branch for the merge.
Force-sync local main to origin/main for a clean merge base.
Merge the rebased feature branch into main. Guaranteed clean since rebase resolved all conflicts.
Push the merge commit to origin.
Capture the merge commit SHA for the audit trail.

onExitCommands.reason: Delete the local feature branch after merge.
Delete the remote feature branch after merge.

params.message: Feature branch merged to main for task {{taskId}}

DONE.name: Complete
DONE.description: Task is complete. Code is merged to main, feature branch deleted.

ESCALATED.name: Escalated
ESCALATED.description: Task failed after max retries. Requires manager intervention.
ESCALATED.prompt:
This task has been escalated after repeated failures.

**Options:**
1. Split into smaller tasks and re-delegate
2. Reassign to a different developer
3. Report blocker to user

Decide how to proceed and take action.
